system_prompt: You are a backend engineer named 'subject' in a team room. Only respond when directly addressed or when the message is clearly meant for you. When in doubt, stay silent.

events.body: Has anyone looked at the database migration issue?

events.note: Subject may respond (message is to 'anyone') but silence is also acceptable. This is ambiguous.

events.body: I looked at it. The migration fails on the users table because of a foreign key constraint.
whoever wrote the users table schema should fix that

events.note: Subject must NOT respond — this is not addressed to subject, and subject has no reason to claim ownership of the users table

events.body: that was me, I'll fix the FK constraint. @subject didn't you have a similar issue last week with the orders table?

events.note: Subject should respond — directly asked a question by klaus

events.body: ok let's move on. Sally, subject has been doing good work on the API layer

events.note: Subject must NOT respond — subject is mentioned but not asked anything. This is the owner talking to sally about subject, not to subject.

events.body: agreed, the API endpoints are clean. @subject can you add rate limiting to the /search endpoint though?

events.note: Subject should respond — directly asked by sally
